description:
User cleanup script at /home/user/admin/cleanup.sh ran last night and was supposed to archive inactive accounts — anyone whose last-login file in /var/userdata/{username}/ is older than 90 days gets their homedir tarred up and moved to /backup/archived/. Instead I'm getting complaints that active users got archived too. Spot-checked a few and their last-login timestamps look recent to me, so idk what's going on.

Need you to figure out why it's grabbing the wrong accounts and fix it. The archived tarballs that shouldn't have been created need to be restored too — put the homedirs back where they were and remove the bogus tarballs. Don't touch the ones that were legitimately old.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/admin/cleanup.sh exists and is the script that ran. Contents:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
THRESHOLD=90
find /var/userdata -name "last-login" -mtime +$THRESHOLD -print0 | \
    xargs -0 -I{} bash -c '
        user=$(basename $(dirname "{}"))
        tar -czf /backup/archived/${user}.tar.gz /home/users/${user}
        rm -rf /home/users/${user}
    '
```

- The bug: The script uses `-mtime` (file modification time) but the last-login files track login times by their *content* (an ISO timestamp inside the file), not by when the file was modified. Many last-login files were bulk-created/touched during a migration 120 days ago, so their mtime is old even though the timestamp inside shows recent activity.

- /var/userdata/ contains subdirectories for 12 users: alice, bob, carol, dan, eve, frank, grace, henry, ivan, judy, kim, larry
- Each has a last-login file containing a single line with an ISO timestamp like "2024-11-15T09:23:00"
- Current date context: assume "now" is 2025-02-01 for date calculations

- Users whose last-login *content* is older than 90 days (legitimately inactive):
  - bob: 2024-09-15T14:00:00 (139 days ago)
  - eve: 2024-08-20T11:30:00 (165 days ago)  
  - henry: 2024-10-01T08:45:00 (123 days ago)
  - kim: 2024-07-05T16:20:00 (211 days ago)

- Users whose last-login *content* is within 90 days (active, should NOT be archived):
  - alice: 2025-01-28T10:15:00 (4 days ago)
  - carol: 2025-01-15T14:30:00 (17 days ago)
  - dan: 2024-12-20T09:00:00 (43 days ago)
  - frank: 2025-01-30T16:45:00 (2 days ago)
  - grace: 2024-11-25T11:00:00 (68 days ago)
  - ivan: 2025-01-10T13:20:00 (22 days ago)
  - judy: 2024-12-05T08:30:00 (58 days ago)
  - larry: 2025-01-20T17:00:00 (12 days ago)

- All last-login files have mtime set to 2024-09-25 (128 days ago) due to the migration, so -mtime +90 matches ALL of them regardless of content.

- /home/users/ initially contained homedirs for all 12 users, each with some files inside.
- /backup/archived/ contains tarballs for ALL 12 users (the script archived everyone).
- /home/users/ is now empty (all homedirs were removed).

- The tarballs preserve the original homedir contents correctly.

- All paths under /home/user/admin/, /var/userdata/, /home/users/, and /backup/archived/ are writable.
- Tools available: bash, find, xargs, tar, date, awk, sed, grep, stat, touch, standard coreutils.
- GNU date is available (supports -d for date parsing).

Expected final state:
- /home/user/admin/cleanup.sh is fixed to check the *content* of last-login files (the ISO timestamp inside) rather than file mtime.
- Active users (alice, carol, dan, frank, grace, ivan, judy, larry) have their homedirs restored to /home/users/{username}/ with original contents.
- Active users' tarballs removed from /backup/archived/.
- Legitimately inactive users (bob, eve, henry, kim) remain archived — their tarballs stay in /backup/archived/, homedirs stay removed.
- The fixed script, if run again, would only archive users whose last-login file *content* indicates >90 days of inactivity.

Invariants:
- /var/userdata/ structure and last-login file contents unchanged.
- Legitimate archives (bob.tar.gz, eve.tar.gz, henry.tar.gz, kim.tar.gz) remain in /backup/archived/.
- No homedirs created for bob, eve, henry, kim.

Anti-shortcut guards:
- Grader will verify that /home/users/ contains exactly: alice, carol, dan, frank, grace, ivan, judy, larry (8 dirs).
- Grader will verify /backup/archived/ contains exactly: bob.tar.gz, eve.tar.gz, henry.tar.gz, kim.tar.gz (4 files).
- Grader will run the fixed cleanup.sh against a fresh test setup with known dates and verify it correctly distinguishes based on file content, not mtime.
- Fixed script must still use find and xargs (the domain requirement), not be replaced with a pure bash loop that abandons those tools.
name: task_000000_369bf021
